creator: alexander.kuehn@nagilum.de
text:
Right, I already forgot about that again. I usually compile almost all  
my ports with gcc48.. My /etc/make.conf has:

WITH_OPENSSL_PORT=yes
CC=gcc48
CXX=g++48
CPP=cpp48
CFLAGS= -Os -O3 -pipe -march=native -mtune=native

And of course:

/usr/local/bin/openssl version -a
OpenSSL 1.0.1f 6 Jan 2014
built on: Thu Jan  9 18:49:30 CET 2014
platform: BSD-x86-elf
options:  bn(64,32) md2(int) rc4(4x,int) des(ptr,risc1,16,long)  
idea(int) blowfish(idx)
compiler: gcc48 -fPIC -DOPENSSL_PIC -DZLIB_SHARED -DZLIB  
-DOPENSSL_THREADS -pthread -D_REENTRANT -DDSO_DLFCN -DHAVE_DLFCN_H  
-Wa,--noexecstack -DL_ENDIAN -DTERMIOS -O3 -fomit-frame-pointer -Wall  
-Os -O3 -pipe -march=native -mtune=native -fno-strict-aliasing  
-DOPENSSL_BN_ASM_PART_WORDS -DOPENSSL_BN_ASM_MONT  
-DOPENSSL_BN_ASM_GF2m -DSHA1_ASM -DSHA256_ASM -DSHA512_ASM -DMD5_ASM  
-DRMD160_ASM -DAES_ASM -DGHASH_ASM
OPENSSLDIR: "/usr/local/openssl"

Zitat von Mathieu Arnold <mat@mat.cc>:

> What do you have in your environment ?
> Do you have a make.conf, what is in it ?
>
> I see you're using gcc 4.8. I haven't tried to build anything with it yet.
>
> Did you build openssl with it too ?
>
> --
> Mathieu Arnold
